services.name: pub.math:absoluteValue
services.description: WmPublic. Returns the absolute value of the input number.

input_parameters.name: num
input_parameters.description: String Number whose absolute value is to be returned.

output_parameters.name: positiveNumber
output_parameters.description: String Absolute value of the input number.

services.name: pub.math:addFloatList
services.description: WmPublic. Adds a list of floating point numbers (represented in a string list) and returns the sum.

input_parameters.name: numList
input_parameters.description: String List Numbers (floating point numbers represented in a string list) to add.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Sum of the numbers in numList. If a sum cannot be produced, value contains one of the following:

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in numList are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:addFloats
services.description: WmPublic. Adds one floating point number (represented as a String) to another and returns the sum.

input_parameters.name: num1
input_parameters.description: String Number to add.

input_parameters.name: num2
input_parameters.description: String Number to add.

input_parameters.name: precision
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Number of decimal places to which the sum will be rounded. The default value is null. The precision parameter, if specified, will override the behavior set by the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property. For information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see the IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Sum of the numbers in num1 and num2. If a sum cannot be produced, value contains one of the following:

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

Use the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property to specify whether the pub.math:addFloats service return the exact result of an operation involving two floating point numbers, the result as calculated by the JVM, or the result based on a fixed number of decimal places. For information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see the IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

services.name: pub.math:addIntList
services.description: WmPublic. Adds a list of integers (represented in a String list) and returns the sum.

input_parameters.name: numList
input_parameters.description: String List Numbers (integers represented as Strings) to add.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Sum of the numbers in numList.

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in numList are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:addInts
services.description: WmPublic. Adds one integer (represented as a String) to another and returns the sum.

input_parameters.name: num1
input_parameters.description: String Number (integer represented as a String) to add.

input_parameters.name: num2
input_parameters.description: String Number (integer represented as a String) to add.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Sum of num1 and num2.

usage_notes: Make sure the result of your calculation is less than 64 bits in width (the maximum width for the long data type). If the result exceeds this limit, it will generate a data overflow.

Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:addObjects
services.description: WmPublic. Adds one java.lang.Number object to another and returns the sum.

input_parameters.name: num1
input_parameters.description: java.lang.Number Number to add. See the Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

input_parameters.name: num2
input_parameters.description: java.lang.Number Number to add. See the Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: java.lang.Number Sum of the numeric values of num1 and num2.

usage_notes: This service accepts the following sub-classes of java.lang.Number: java.lang.Byte, java.lang.Double, java.lang.Float, java.lang.Integer, java.lang.Long, java.lang.Short.
This service applies the following rules for binary numeric promotion to the operands in order:
These promotion rules mirror the Java rules for numeric promotion of numeric types.

services.name: pub.math:divideFloats
services.description: WmPublic. Divides one floating point number (represented as a String) by another (num1/num2) and returns the quotient.

input_parameters.name: num1
input_parameters.description: String Number (floating point number represented as a String) that is the dividend.

input_parameters.name: num2
input_parameters.description: String Number (floating point number represented as a String) that is the divisor.

input_parameters.name: precision
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Number of decimal places to which the quotient will be rounded. The default value is null. The precision parameter, if specified, will override the behavior set by the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property. For information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see the IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String The quotient of num1 / num2. If a quotient cannot be produced, value contains one of the following:

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

Use the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property to specify whether the pub.math:divideFloats service return the exact result of an operation involving two floating point numbers, the result as calculated by the JVM, or the result based on a fixed number of decimal places. For information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see the IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

services.name: pub.math:divideInts
services.description: WmPublic. Divides one integer (represented as a String) by another (num1/num2) and returns the quotient.

input_parameters.name: num1
input_parameters.description: String Number (integer represented as a String) that is the dividend.

input_parameters.name: num2
input_parameters.description: String Number (integer represented as a String) that is the divisor.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String The quotient of num1 / num2.

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:divideObjects
services.description: WmPublic. Divides one java.lang.Number object by another (num1/num2) and returns the quotient.

input_parameters.name: num1
input_parameters.description: java.lang.Number Number that is the dividend. See the Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

input_parameters.name: num2
input_parameters.description: java.lang.Number Number that is the divisor. See the Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: java.lang.Number Quotient of num1 / num2.

usage_notes: This service accepts the following sub-classes of java.lang.Number: java.lang.Byte, java.lang.Double, java.lang.Float, java.lang.Integer, java.lang.Long, java.lang.Short.
This service applies the following rules for binary numeric promotion to the operands in order:
These promotion rules mirror the Java rules for numeric promotion of numeric types.

services.name: pub.math:max
services.description: WmPublic. Returns the largest number from a list of numbers.

input_parameters.name: numList
input_parameters.description: String List List of numbers from which the largest number is to be returned.

output_parameters.name: maxValue
output_parameters.description: String Largest number from the list of numbers.

services.name: pub.math:min
services.description: WmPublic. Returns smallest number from a list of numbers.

input_parameters.name: numList
input_parameters.description: String List List of numbers from which the smallest number is to be returned.

output_parameters.name: minValue
output_parameters.description: String Smallest number from the list of numbers.

input_parameters.description: String List Numbers (floating point numbers represented as Strings) to multiply.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Product of the numbers in numlist. If a product cannot be produced, value contains one of the following:

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in numList are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

output_parameters.name: value

For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:randomDouble
services.description: WmPublic. Returns the next pseudorandom, uniformly distributed double between 0.0 and 1.0.

output_parameters.name: number
output_parameters.description: String Generated random number.

services.name: pub.math:roundNumber
services.description: WmPublic. Returns a rounded number.

input_parameters.name: num
input_parameters.description: String Number to be rounded.

input_parameters.name: numberOfDigits
input_parameters.description: String Specifies the number of digits to which you want to round the number.

input_parameters.name: roundingMode
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies the rounding method. Valid values for the roundingMode parameter are RoundHalfUp, RoundUp, RoundDown, RoundCeiling, RoundFloor, RoundHalfDown, and RoundHalfEven. The default value is RoundHalfUp.

output_parameters.name: roundedNumber
output_parameters.description: String The rounded number.

services.name: pub.math:subtractFloats
services.description: WmPublic. Subtracts one floating point number (represented as a String) from another and returns the difference.

input_parameters.name: num1
input_parameters.description: String Number (floating point number represented as a String).

input_parameters.name: num2
input_parameters.description: String Number (floating point number represented as a String) to subtract from num1.

input_parameters.name: precision
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Number of decimal places to which the difference will be rounded. The default value is null. The precision parameter, if specified, will override the behavior set by the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property. For information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see the IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Difference of num1 - num2. If a difference cannot be produced, value contains one of the following:

usage_notes: Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

Use the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property to specify whether the pub.math:subtractFloats service return the exact result of an operation involving two floating point numbers, the result as calculated by the JVM, or the result based on a fixed number of decimal places. For more information about the watt.server.math.floatOperation.mode property, see IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide

services.name: pub.math:subtractInts
services.description: WmPublic. Subtracts one integer (represented as a String) from another and returns the difference.

input_parameters.name: num1
input_parameters.description: String Number (integer represented as a String).

input_parameters.name: num2
input_parameters.description: String Number (integer represented as a String) to subtract from num1.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Difference of num1 - num2.

usage_notes: Make sure the result of your calculation is less than 64 bits in width (the maximum width for the long data type). If the result exceeds this limit, it will generate a data overflow.

Make sure the strings that are passed to the service in num1 andnum2 are in a locale-neutral format (that is, using the pattern -####.##). Passing locally formatted strings may result in unexpected results. For example, calling pub.math:addFloats in a German locale with the arguments 1,23 and 2,34 will result in the value 357, not 3.57 or 3,57.

services.name: pub.math:subtractObjects
services.description: WmPublic. Subtracts one java.lang.Number object from another and returns the difference.

input_parameters.name: num1
input_parameters.description: java.lang.Number Number. See the Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

input_parameters.name: num2
input_parameters.description: java.lang.Number Number to subtract from num1. See Usage Notes for supported sub-classes.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: java.lang.Number Difference of num1 - num2.

usage_notes: This service accepts the following sub-classes of java.lang.Number: java.lang.Byte, java.lang.Double, java.lang.Float, java.lang.Integer, java.lang.Long, java.lang.Short.
This service applies the following rules for binary numeric promotion to the operands. The following rules are applied in order:
These promotion rules mirror the Java rules for numeric promotion of numeric types.

services.name: pub.math:toNumber
services.description: WmPublic. Converts a string to numeric data type.

input_parameters.name: num
input_parameters.description: String Number (represented as a string) to be converted to numeric format.

input_parameters.name: convertAs
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies the Java numeric data type to which the num parameter is to be converted. Valid values for the convertAs parameter are java.lang.Double, java.lang.Float, java.lang.Integer,java.math.BigDecimal,java.math.BigInteger, java.lang.Long. The default value is java.lang.Double.

output_parameters.name: num
output_parameters.description: java.lang.Number Converted numeric object.
